methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

private.name: Shinkei
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Whisper Aero
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Daylight Computer is a Series A consumer technology company founded by Anjan Katta that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a limited comparable universe of two private peers (Shinkei and Whisper Aero) with no public market comps available in the consumer tech sector for direct benchmarking.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($10M) as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $20M. This method assumes comparable risk profiles and capital efficiency across the target and peer cohort, though limited comp density restricts confidence in the multiplier assumption.

The triangulated valuation range of $17M (low) to $23M (high), with a median of $20M, reflects uncertainty inherent in single-method analysis. The $6M spread between low and high estimates captures plausible variance in step-up assumptions and company-specific execution risk. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of available private comparables, the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this specific company stage and sector dynamics, and absence of public market validation. Investors should note that this range is supported by limited methodological diversity and would benefit from additional revenue, unit economics, or market-size data to triangulate more robustly.
